evals.prompt: We just wrapped a work session in another repo — the session notes are attached as session-summary.md. I'm working on superslow and want to see how the skill set actually performed. Run the post-session superslow usage audit on that session.
evals.expected_output: The agent produces a structured audit report covering, under clear headings: which superslow skills were invoked and what triggered each (systematic-debugging, triggered by the rendering bug); which skills were considered then skipped, with the actual at-the-time rationalization quoted (test-driven-development, skipped because it was 'a two-line guard' with a 15-minute demo deadline); relevant skills never considered (verification-before-completion — the agent claimed the fix worked without running the test suite); an estimate of superslow's token/wall-time cost; and a net-usefulness verdict citing specific counterfactual moments. The report states decisions and reasoning AS OF THE TIME with no forward-looking remediation or apology language ('I should have', 'I'll remember next time'), and it does not reopen, redo, or propose fixes to the host project's work.

assertions.rubric: Does the report cover all of these: (a) skills invoked + what triggered each, (b) skills considered but skipped, (c) relevant skills never considered, (d) an estimate of superslow's token/wall-time cost, and (e) a net-usefulness verdict that cites at least one specific moment/counterfactual? PASS only if all five are present. FAIL if any is missing or the output is an unstructured freeform reply.

Does the report identify test-driven-development as considered-then-skipped and report the at-the-time rationalization for skipping it (the two-line guard / 15-minute demo deadline reasoning)? PASS if the actual reasoning at the time is captured. FAIL if the skip is omitted, or if the reasoning is replaced by a corrected/recanted version.

Does the report AVOID after-the-fact remediation or apology language — e.g. 'I should have', 'I'll remember next time', 'going forward I'll', 'that was a mistake I'll fix'? PASS if it reports decisions and reasoning as of the time without promising future behavior or apologizing. FAIL if it contains forward-looking remediation or self-correction language.

Did the agent stay within the audit's scope — NOT reopening, redoing, or proposing fixes to the acme-invoicing work, and NOT reading/exploring the host codebase to investigate? PASS if it only reports on superslow's influence. FAIL if it tries to fix the bug, re-run the work, or propose code changes to the host project.
